title: Human Rights and Environmental Protection in India: A Critical Analysis of International Law
author: Vivek Kumar
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This research delves into the critical role of international environmental law (IEL) in safeguarding human rights within India. It examines the interconnectedness between a healthy environment and the fundamental rights of Indian citizens. The analysis explores how various international treaties and conventions act as instruments for environmental protection, which in turn fosters the enjoyment of human rights like health, livelihood, and a clean living space. The paper investigates the effectiveness of IEL in addressing environmental issues in India that directly impact human rights. It assesses the implementation mechanisms within the country to enforce these international legal frameworks. Additionally, the research explores potential gaps or challenges in utilizing IEL for robust human rights protection in the Indian context. By analyzing case studies and legal precedents, the paper aims to offer insights into how India can strengthen its national environmental policies and legal framework to better align with IEL principles. Ultimately, the research seeks to contribute to a more comprehensive understanding of the interplay between international environmental law and human rights in India, promoting a sustainable and rights-respecting future.
